lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: What bloody man is that? He can report,

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: As seemeth by his plight, of the revolt

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: The newest state.

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: This is the sergeant

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: Who like a good and hardy soldier fought

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: ’Gainst my captivity. Hail, brave friend!

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: Say to the king the knowledge of the broil

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: As thou didst leave it.

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Doubtful it stood;

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: As two spent swimmers, that do cling together

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: And choke their art. The merciless Macdonwald—

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Worthy to be a rebel, for to that

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: The multiplying villainies of nature

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Do swarm upon him—from the western isles

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Of kerns and gallowglasses is supplied;

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: And fortune, on his damned quarrel smiling,

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Show’d like a rebel’s whore: but all’s too weak:

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: For brave Macbeth—well he deserves that name—

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Disdaining fortune, with his brandish’d steel,

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Which smoked with bloody execution,

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Like valour’s minion carved out his passage

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Till he faced the slave;

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Which ne’er shook hands, nor bade farewell to him,

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Till he unseam’d him from the nave to the chaps,

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: And fix’d his head upon our battlements.

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: O valiant cousin! worthy gentleman!

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: As whence the sun ’gins his reflection

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Shipwrecking storms and direful thunders break,

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: So from that spring whence comfort seem’d to come

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Discomfort swells. Mark, king of Scotland, mark:

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: No sooner justice had with valour arm’d

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Compell’d these skipping kerns to trust their heels,

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: But the Norweyan lord surveying vantage,

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: With furbish’d arms and new supplies of men

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Began a fresh assault.

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Dismay’d not this

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Our captains, Macbeth and Banquo?

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Yes;

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: As sparrows eagles, or the hare the lion.

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: If I say sooth, I must report they were

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: As cannons overcharged with double cracks, so they

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Doubly redoubled strokes upon the foe:

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Except they meant to bathe in reeking wounds,

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: Or memorize another Golgotha,

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: I cannot tell.

lines.speaker: SERGEANT
lines.text: But I am faint, my gashes cry for help.

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: So well thy words become thee as thy wounds;

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: They smack of honour both. Go get him surgeons.

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Who comes here?

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: The worthy thane of Ross.

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: What a haste looks through his eyes! So should he look

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: That seems to speak things strange.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: God save the king!

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Whence camest thou, worthy thane?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: From Fife, great king;

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Where the Norweyan banners flout the sky

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: And fan our people cold. Norway himself,

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: With terrible numbers,

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Assisted by that most disloyal traitor

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: The thane of Cawdor, began a dismal conflict;

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Till that Bellona’s bridegroom, lapp’d in proof,

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Confronted him with self-comparisons,

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Point against point rebellious, arm ’gainst arm,

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Curbing his lavish spirit: and, to conclude,

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: The victory fell on us.

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Great happiness!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: That now

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Sweno, the Norways’ king, craves composition;

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Nor would we deign him burial of his men

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Till he disbursed at Saint Colme’s inch

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ten thousand dollars to our general use.

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: No more that thane of Cawdor shall deceive

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Our bosom interest: go pronounce his present death,

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: And with his former title greet Macbeth.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I’ll see it done.

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: What he hath lost noble Macbeth hath won.

stage_directions.text: Alarum within. Enter Duncan, Malcolm, Donalbain, Lennox, with Attendants, meeting a bleeding Sergeant.

glosses.definition: here, a senior soldier; a wounded officer
light-armed Irish foot soldiers and heavy-armed mercenaries

glosses.phrase: Fortune, on his damned quarrel smiling
glosses.definition: Lady Luck favoring his evil cause
fickle; unfaithful — Fortune figured as faithless

glosses.phrase: unseam’d him from the nave to the chaps
glosses.definition: split him open from the navel to the jaws — graphic battle-image for Macbeth's ferocity

glosses.phrase: As whence the sun ’gins his reflection
glosses.definition: just as in the direction the sun rises

glosses.phrase: So from that spring whence comfort seem’d to come
glosses.definition: from the source that seemed to bring relief
Macbeth figured as the husband of Bellona, Roman goddess of war — high praise
equal matching; meeting him blow for blow
Inchcolm, an island in the Firth of Forth used as a burial ground
Scottish nobleman; Cawdor is a region in the Highlands — Thane is below an earl in rank

glosses.phrase: What he hath lost noble Macbeth hath won
glosses.definition: the title Macbeth is about to inherit — and the ambition that comes with it

annotations.title: Macbeth as Bellona's bridegroom: the problem of praise
annotations.body:
The bleeding Captain's report introduces Macbeth not as a man but as a killing machine. He 'unseam'd him from the nave to the chaps' — a graphic battlefield image that lets the audience see Macbeth's capacity for violence before he ever enters. The Captain's praise is extravagant ('Bellona's bridegroom'), figuring Macbeth as the husband of the Roman war goddess.

The praise matters structurally. What we will later see Macbeth do — to Duncan, Banquo, Macduff's family — he first did on the battlefield, and he was rewarded for it. The play's moral economy refuses a clean line between the sanctioned violence of war and the prohibited violence of murder. Macbeth's skill at one made his access to the other possible.

Shakespeare does not sentimentalize. The Captain's tongue-in-cheek horror at Macbeth's surgery with a sword tells the audience what kind of heroic economy Scotland runs on.
sources: Stephen Greenblatt, *Will in the World* (2004), ch. 12

annotations.title: The Thane of Cawdor's title, and its transfer
annotations.body:
Duncan's royal act — stripping a traitor of his title and bestowing it on a loyal subject — is presented as swift, clean, legitimate. But the play will spend its next five acts exposing how fragile that transfer is. Titles, the play will show, are clothing: they fit only the man for whom they were cut.

The 'Thane of Cawdor' is about to become, in rapid succession, three different men: the executed traitor, Macbeth the newly loyal, Macbeth the regicide. Shakespeare is careful: the title never really belongs to Macbeth, and the play's clothing imagery (culminating in the 'giant's robe upon a dwarfish thief' in 5.2) begins here.
sources: Cleanth Brooks, 'The Naked Babe and the Cloak of Manliness' (1947)

trials.prompt: What does the Captain report about Macbeth's defeat of Macdonwald?
options: Macbeth killed Macdonwald in single combat by splitting him from navel to jaws
Macbeth captured him and demanded ransom
Figures him as husband of the Roman war goddess — a high, violent praise

trials.prompt: Why does Shakespeare open the play with a military report celebrating Macbeth's violence?
options: To bore the audience with exposition
To establish that Macbeth's capacity for murder was honored before it was feared — the transfer from soldier to regicide is shorter than it looks
